name: Reconco and Fontalbres Loop from Onil
name_original: Las trialeras del Reconco — Circular desde Onil hasta Fontalbres
description: This moderate 17.7-mile (28.4 km) mountain biking route from Onil to Fontalbres offers varied terrain and panoramic views from Reconco summi
region_intro: Mountain biking around Onil is characterized by its rugged relief, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Onil mountain range. The region features both mountainous areas and plains, providing diverse terrain for mountain bike trails. Elevations in the Sierra de Onil can exceed 1000 meters, offering varied challenges and panoramic views. This landscape includes well-maintained paths and natural trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Onil The most popular mountain bike trail is Reconco and Fontalbres Loop from Onil, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views from the Reconco summit. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cuatro Caminos – Cami del Xocolatero Trail loop from Onil, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.8 km) path. This route navigates through diverse landscapes, showcasing varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Cuatro Caminos – Puerto de Canalís d'Onil loop from Onil, a 17.4 miles (28.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Onil, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Onil is defined by the Sierra de Onil mountain range, its rugged relief, and the contrast between mountainous areas and plains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Onil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Onil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Onil?
A: Onil offers a diverse network of over 20 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Onil?
A: Mountain biking in Onil features a rugged relief, with trails winding through both mountainous areas and plains at the foot of the Sierra de Onil. You'll encounter varied terrain, including well-maintained paths and natural trails, with elevations that can exceed 1000 meters, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for beginners in Onil?
A: Yes, Onil has options for less experienced riders. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Onil?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Onil offers 8 difficult mountain bike routes. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31574524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuatro Caminos – Racó of Saint Bonaventure loop from Onil&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 51 km and features significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see while mountain biking in Onil?
A: The trails around Onil offer access to several natural highlights. You might encounter the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3994612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Menejador&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1023814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Font Roja&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4752714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant's Trunk Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3744444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Mariola Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent natural feature in the area.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles near the mountain bike trails in Onil?
A: Yes, the region around Onil is rich in history. You can explore impressive castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7243255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3621301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castalla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3097812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barchell Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which are all within reach and add a cultural dimension to your mountain biking adventure.

Q: What do other mountain bikers appreciate about the trails in Onil?
A: The mountain bike trails in Onil are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 out of 5 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels, and the panoramic views, especially from summits like Reconco.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in Onil?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, Onil offers several routes exceeding 25 km. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33873926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuatro Caminos – Cami del Xocolatero Trail loop from Onil&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 39 km, or the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31574524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuatro Caminos – Racó of Saint Bonaventure loop from Onil&lt;/a&gt; at over 51 km.

Q: Can I find loop trails for mountain biking in Onil?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Onil are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927436687/reconco-and-fontalbres-loop-from-onil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reconco and Fontalbres Loop from Onil&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31484099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuatro Caminos – Puerto de Canalís d'Onil loop from Onil&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Onil?
A: Given Onil's location in the Alcoià region, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring cooler weather, especially at higher elevations in the Sierra de Onil.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several trails provide stunning panoramic views, particularly from higher points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927436687/reconco-and-fontalbres-loop-from-onil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reconco and Fontalbres Loop from Onil&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its panoramic views from the Reconco summit, showcasing the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Onil.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access The Reconco trails - Circular from Onil to Fontalbres?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of Onil, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Look for public parking areas within the town center or near the Coto Escolar park, which is a common access point for trails in the area.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Onil are available, primarily by bus. Services like Vectalia often connect Onil with larger towns in the Alicante province. Check local bus schedules for routes that stop at locations like the Coto Escolar park or the town center in Onil, which are convenient for accessing the trail.

faqs.question: Is The Reconco trails - Circular from Onil to Fontalbres dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or in areas with sensitive flora and fauna. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Reconco trails are best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in the Alicante province, making midday rides challenging. Winters are generally mild, but occasional rain can make some sections muddy. Spring offers pleasant weather and blooming flora, while autumn provides comfortable temperatures and clear views.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for mountain biking on these trails?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a specific permit or have to pay an entrance fee to mountain bike on the Reconco trails. The area is generally open for public recreational use. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Reconco trails from Onil to Fontalbres?
faqs.answer: This route offers a varied mountain biking experience. You can expect a mix of singletracks, wider tracks, and some roads. The trail traverses the sunny side of the Onil mountain range and crosses the Tormo ravine. Some segments involve steeper inclines as it ascends the ridge of the Reconco mountain range, providing a moderate challenge.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several interesting points. You'll cross the Barranco del Tormo, where you can observe fossils of nummulites. There's also a botanical trail, particularly around the Peña Buitrera micro-reserve of flora, with information panels detailing local plant species. A significant highlight is the Source of the Fontalbres Stream. As you climb to the Reconco summit, you'll be rewarded with panoramic views of the Alicante province.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 28.4 km (17.6 miles) long with about 839 meters (2,753 feet) of elevation gain. For a mountain biker of moderate fitness, it typically takes around 3 hours and 5 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on skill level and stops for scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route family-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, varied terrain including singletracks and significant climbs, this route might be challenging for very young children or beginners. It's more suitable for families with older children or teenagers who have some mountain biking experience and are comfortable with moderate ascents and descents.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or accommodation options near the trailhead in Onil?
faqs.answer: Yes, Onil is a town with amenities. You can find various cafes, restaurants, and pubs in the town center where you can refuel before or after your ride. For accommodation, there are typically local guesthouses or small hotels available in Onil or nearby towns.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Santuario - Fontalbres - Sierra de la Fontanella - Santuario, El Camí IT5TR4 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, and Calle Monca.
